title: SICP 5.3 storage allocation and GC model
goal: Implement the SICP 5.3 storage allocation and garbage-collector model as a runnable Eshkol probe, covering vector-modeled memory, pair allocation, free pointer behavior, root traversal, and relocation invariants.
next_actions: Add tests/sicp/ch5_storage_gc.esk with the SICP memory-vector model for cons cells.
Model the-cars, the-cdrs, free pointer allocation, and pointer-tagged pair references.
Add allocation checks for list construction and mutation.
Implement a compact GC model sufficient to check root preservation, relocation, and free-space recovery.
Keep the probe deterministic and bounded so it runs under both JIT and AOT smoke guards.
acceptance: tests/sicp/ch5_storage_gc.esk exists and self-checks the SICP 5.3 storage/GC model.
The probe passes under AOT compile/run.
ICC sicp-completeness observes sicp_ch5_storage_gc_r and sicp_ch5_storage_gc_aot as PASS.
ux_payoff: Closes the book's explicit memory-model chapter and gives Eshkol a concrete simulator-level GC regression.
